sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: RC Sporting Charleroi have struggled recently, managing only one win in their last five matches, with a mix of draws and losses (1W-2D-2L). KRC Genk are also in poor form, failing to secure a win in their last four matches (0W-2D-2L), including a heavy 3-0 defeat to Royal Antwerp. Genk's inability to keep a clean sheet in their last nine games is a notable concern.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Charleroi secured a narrow 1-0 victory in September 2025, while the earlier match in February 2025 ended in a 1-1 draw. Recent head-to-head stats suggest low-scoring games, with under 2.5 goals in 4 of their last 5 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Charleroi, sitting 12th with 20 points, have scored 19 goals this season (12th in the league) and conceded 23 (9th for least conceded). Genk, in 7th with 24 points, have a slightly better offensive output with 22 goals but have conceded 24, showing defensive vulnerabilities. Charleroi's strength in ground duels (3rd in the league at 53.3%) could play a role against Genk's possession-heavy style.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Charleroi, Parfait Guiagon (4 goals, 2 assists, 7.11 avg. rating) is a standout in midfield, ranking 1st in the league for successful dribbles per game. Genk's Jarne Steuckers, despite not scoring this season, has 5 assists (2nd in the league) and could be pivotal with his playmaking ability (10 big chances created, 2nd in the league).

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given the balanced probabilities from SofaScore (36% home win, 28% draw, 36% away win) and both teams' recent struggles, this match is likely to be tight. Charleroi's home advantage and Genk's defensive frailties tilt the prediction slightly in favor of a narrow home win, though a draw remains a strong possibility.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between RC Sporting Charleroi and KRC Genk ended in a 1-1 draw at Stade du Pays de Charleroi. Both teams showed moments of promise, with Genk taking the lead early through Bryan Heynen before Charleroi responded late in the half via Yassine Titraoui. The scoreline reflects a balanced contest, with neither side able to dominate for extended periods. Genk held more possession, while Charleroi showed resilience to fight back.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attacking momentum
halftimeSections.text: The attacking momentum in the first half saw significant shifts between the two teams. Genk started strongly, controlling the early phases with consistent attacking spells and creating pressure in the opening minutes. Their persistence paid off with a goal, maintaining their threat for much of the first half. However, Charleroi gradually found their footing, with momentum swinging in their favor during the latter stages. Their increased intensity led to several dangerous moments, culminating in the equalizer just before the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team statistics breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Genk enjoyed a higher share of possession in the first half and recorded more shots on target compared to Charleroi. Their ability to win possession in the final third also gave them an edge in creating opportunities. On the other hand, Charleroi were more effective in aerial duels and overall duels, showcasing physicality and determination. Their higher number of corner kicks suggests they were able to push forward and create set-piece chances. Both teams had similar entries into the penalty area, indicating a closely contested battle in the attacking zones.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: For Charleroi, Yassine Titraoui stood out with his goal and overall impact in the attack, proving to be a pivotal figure in their fightback. His composure in finding the net late in the half could inspire confidence for the remainder of the game. On Genk's side, Bryan Heynen was instrumental, not only scoring the opener but also contributing defensively. His all-around performance has been a highlight, while Daan Heymans' assist underlines his importance in creating chances for his team.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, both teams have reasons to believe they can take control of the game. Charleroi will likely build on their late momentum from the first half, aiming to exploit set-pieces and aerial dominance. Genk, with their superior possession play, might look to dictate the tempo and capitalize on their ability to win the ball in dangerous areas. Given the open nature of the first half, we can expect more goal-scoring opportunities as fatigue sets in and spaces open up. Charleroi's home advantage could play a role, but Genk's attacking intent might give them the edge if they sharpen their finishing.
